scenario_title: SOA serial change on dynamic update is unspecified
test_rationale: This test performs one ordinary RFC 2136 UPDATE that adds a new in-zone A record and then queries the zone apex SOA. The UPDATE does not touch the SOA directly, so any serial change is an implementation policy choice rather than a protocol requirement. Because BIND and Knot can differ on whether they auto-increment the SOA serial after a successful update, the post-update SOA query provides a compact observable oracle for the underspecified behavior.

group_1.behavior: Automatically increments the SOA serial after the successful UPDATE.
group_1.post_state: apex SOA serial changes from 3 to a higher value (typically 4, but implementation policy may vary).

group_2.behavior: Leaves the SOA serial unchanged unless the SOA itself is explicitly modified by the UPDATE.

expected_divergence.why_divergence: RFC 2136 specifies how UPDATEs modify zone data but does not define any mandatory SOA-serial bump policy, arithmetic, or timing for successful updates that do not explicitly edit the SOA. As a result, authoritative servers may legally differ on whether the apex SOA serial changes after an ordinary update.
